title: cyperus triceps var. obtusiflorus
intro: Cyperus triceps var. obtusiflorus, also known as the obtuse-flowered umbrella sedge, is a type of flowering plant in the sedge family (Cyperaceae) that is native to tropical Africa. The plant has been found to have various medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities. The health benefits of Cyperus triceps var. obtusiflorus include its use in traditional medicine to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness its medicinal properties, although more research is needed to fully understand its effects on human health.
